questions.q: Angada belongs to which race of beings in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Angada is a vanara prince of the monkey-folk of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Whose son is Angada?
questions.explanation: Angada is the son of the mighty vanara king Vali.

questions.q: Who is Angada's mother?
questions.explanation: Angada's mother is Tara, the wise queen of Kishkindha.

questions.q: In which army did Angada serve as a commander?
questions.explanation: Angada became a chief commander in Rama's vanara army against Ravana.

questions.q: Angada served as Rama's envoy to whose court?
questions.explanation: Angada was sent as an envoy to deliver Rama's ultimatum at Ravana's court in Lanka.

questions.q: Vali, Angada's father, was the brother of which vanara king?
questions.explanation: Vali was the elder brother of Sugriva, both kings of Kishkindha.

questions.q: Angada is described as which stage of life when he joins the epic's events?
questions.explanation: Angada is portrayed as a young vanara prince, valiant and energetic.

questions.q: What was the purpose of the great vanara army that Angada served in?
questions.explanation: The vanara army was raised to help Rama find and rescue Sita from Lanka.

questions.q: Angada is the nephew of which vanara king through his father?
questions.explanation: Since Vali and Sugriva are brothers, Angada is Sugriva's nephew.

questions.q: The kingdom of Kishkindha, ruled by Angada's family, was a realm of what kind of terrain?
questions.explanation: Kishkindha was a mountain realm of caves and forests inhabited by the vanaras.

questions.q: After Vali's death, who became king of Kishkindha rather than Angada?
questions.explanation: Sugriva ascended the throne of Kishkindha after Vali was slain by Rama's arrow.

questions.q: Following Vali's death, what position was Angada granted in Kishkindha?
options: He was given the throne immediately
questions.explanation: Angada was installed as the crown prince, heir to Sugriva, after his father's death.

questions.q: Angada was appointed to lead the search for Sita in which direction?
questions.explanation: Sugriva sent Angada to lead the search party toward the south, where Sita was eventually found.

questions.q: Which famous devotee of Rama accompanied Angada in the southern search party?
questions.explanation: Hanuman traveled with Angada's southern search party that ultimately located Sita.

questions.q: At Ravana's court, Angada planted his foot on the ground and challenged the rakshasas to do what?
options: Move his foot from the ground
questions.explanation: Angada challenged Ravana's warriors to merely move his planted foot, and none could budge it.

questions.q: What was the outcome when Ravana's warriors tried to move Angada's planted foot?
options: None of them could budge it
questions.explanation: Not even Ravana's mightiest warriors could move Angada's foot, humbling the demon court.

questions.q: What was the chief message Angada carried to Ravana as Rama's envoy?
options: A demand to return Sita or face destruction
questions.explanation: Angada delivered Rama's ultimatum that Ravana surrender Sita or be destroyed in battle.

questions.q: When the southern search party despaired at the ocean's edge, who reminded them of his power to leap across?
questions.explanation: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his forgotten powers, and Hanuman then leapt the ocean to Lanka.

questions.q: The aged vulture who told Angada's search party that Sita was in Lanka was named what?
questions.explanation: Sampati, brother of Jatayu, informed the southern search party that Sita was held in Lanka.

questions.q: Which weapon or emblem is most associated with Angada in his depictions?
questions.explanation: Angada is associated with the mace, like his father Vali and other mighty vanara warriors.

questions.q: Angada's mission to Ravana's court took place at which stage of the story?
options: Just before the war on Lanka
questions.explanation: Angada's embassy to Ravana came as a final attempt at peace just before the great battle.

questions.q: Which vanara king did Rama kill, leading to Angada's father's death?
questions.explanation: Rama slew Vali, Angada's father, with an arrow to restore Sugriva to the throne.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Valmiki Ramayana does Angada first prominently appear amid the vanaras of Kishkindha?
questions.explanation: Angada features prominently in the Kishkindha Kanda among the vanaras allied with Rama.

questions.q: When the southern search party feared they had failed and considered fasting to death, who delivered a rousing speech about persevering or losing their lives to Sugriva?
questions.explanation: Angada led the despairing search party, debating their fate before Sampati's news renewed their hope.

questions.q: Through Tara and Vali, Angada is linked to a divine lineage; Vali was said to be the son of which deity?
questions.explanation: Vali was traditionally regarded as the son of Indra, making Angada Indra's grandson.

questions.q: During his embassy, when Angada offered his foot challenge, what did he imply if the rakshasas could move it?
options: Rama would withdraw and Sita would stay
questions.explanation: Angada declared that if his foot could be moved, Rama would give up Sita and return, underscoring vanara might.

questions.q: Whose son did Angada confront or rebuke among Ravana's court when belittled, asserting vanara valor?
questions.explanation: Angada boldly defied Ravana's sons and ministers at court, demonstrating fearless valor as an envoy.

questions.q: At the end of the epic, what becomes of Angada in the tradition of the Ramayana?
options: He remains crown prince and later succeeds to rule Kishkindha
He renounces the world entirely first
He is slain in the war
questions.explanation: Angada survives the war and continues as crown prince of Kishkindha, in line to rule after Sugriva.

questions.q: Which gift did Rama bestow upon Angada and the vanaras in gratitude after the victory at Lanka?
options: Honors, jewels, and his abiding friendship
questions.explanation: Rama honored Angada and the vanaras with jewels, praise, and lasting friendship after the war.

questions.q: Angada's grandmother on his mother's side connects him to which vanara physician of the war?
questions.explanation: Tara was the daughter of Sushena, the vanara physician who healed warriors during the war on Lanka.
